etymology.description: semantic 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 牚(牚) (chēng)
etymology.interpretation: 撐(撐) (chēng, "support; prop up") combines 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") as the semantic element, indicating an action done by hand, with 牚(牚) as the phonetic part providing the sound. This suggests the action of using hands to hold something up or keep it in place, directly tied to the meaning "support; prop up."
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 撐 is simplified in form through script simplification, which is not explained through descriptions of phonetically or semantically related components.

examples.chinese: 他用木棍撑住门(他用木棍撐住門)。
examples.english: He propped the door open with a stick.

examples.chinese: 这把椅子可以撑很多重量(這把椅子可以撐很多重量)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè bǎ yǐzi kěyǐ chēng hěnduō zhòngliàng.
examples.english: This chair can support a lot of weight.

examples.chinese: 我们必须互相撑着(我們必須互相撐著)，度过这些难关(度過這些難關)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bìxū hùxiāng chēngzhe, dùguò zhèxiē nánguān.
examples.english: We must support each other to get through these difficult times.
